evidence: Delta-State CRDTs: "small messages with an incremental nature" (Almeida et al., 2014).
claims.claim: Delta-state CRDTs can disseminate small incremental deltas over unreliable channels while retaining state-based convergence properties.
claims.title: Delta-state CRDTs reduce sync bandwidth vs full-state anti-entropy
claims.name: Delta-state CRDTs reduce sync bandwidth vs full-state anti-entropy
claims.text: Delta-state CRDTs can disseminate small incremental deltas over unreliable channels while retaining state-based convergence properties.

evidence: CRDTs: "any replica can be modified without coordinating"; replicas with same updates "reach the same state" (Preguica et al., 2018).
claims.claim: CRDTs are designed so replicas can be modified without coordination and converge deterministically once they have the same set of updates.
claims.title: CRDTs allow coordination-free local updates with deterministic convergence
claims.name: CRDTs allow coordination-free local updates with deterministic convergence
claims.text: CRDTs are designed so replicas can be modified without coordination and converge deterministically once they have the same set of updates.

evidence: Demers et al.: "back up rumor mongering with anti-entropy" to "guarantee complete coverage".
claims.claim: Gossip-style rumor mongering may fail to deliver updates to some nodes; backing it with anti-entropy can guarantee complete coverage.
claims.title: Rumor mongering alone can miss nodes; anti-entropy backup is used for coverage
claims.name: Rumor mongering alone can miss nodes; anti-entropy backup is used for coverage
claims.text: Gossip-style rumor mongering may fail to deliver updates to some nodes; backing it with anti-entropy can guarantee complete coverage.

evidence: SSB selective replication: as system scales, per-peer data to handle "stays roughly the same" (Tarr et al., 2019).
claims.claim: Selective replication driven by a social graph can keep per-peer data volume roughly stable as the overall network scales.
claims.title: Selective replication based on social graph can bound per-peer interest set
claims.name: Selective replication based on social graph can bound per-peer interest set
claims.text: Selective replication driven by a social graph can keep per-peer data volume roughly stable as the overall network scales.

evidence: RFC 3972: interface identifier generated by hashing "a public key and auxiliary parameters"; works without PKI.
claims.claim: CGA (RFC 3972) describes generating an IPv6 interface identifier by hashing a public key and parameters, enabling verification without certificate authorities.
claims.title: CGA binds a public key to an IPv6 address without PKI
claims.name: CGA binds a public key to an IPv6 address without PKI
claims.text: CGA (RFC 3972) describes generating an IPv6 interface identifier by hashing a public key and parameters, enabling verification without certificate authorities.

evidence: Pocket Switched Networks: inter-contact time "follows an approximate power law" (Chaintreau et al., 2005).
claims.claim: Empirical mobility traces can show inter-contact time distributions closer to power laws than exponentials, impacting opportunistic forwarding assumptions.
claims.title: Inter-contact times can follow a power law, affecting forwarding strategy
claims.name: Inter-contact times can follow a power law, affecting forwarding strategy
claims.text: Empirical mobility traces can show inter-contact time distributions closer to power laws than exponentials, impacting opportunistic forwarding assumptions.

evidence: Opportunistic/DTN: "store and forward service" in a "mostly disconnected network" (Palazzo et al., 2011).
claims.claim: Opportunistic networks often cannot assume a continuously connected path and instead provide store-and-forward with infrequent contact times.
claims.title: Opportunistic/DTN networks commonly provide store-and-forward in disconnected graphs
claims.name: Opportunistic/DTN networks commonly provide store-and-forward in disconnected graphs
claims.text: Opportunistic networks often cannot assume a continuously connected path and instead provide store-and-forward with infrequent contact times.

evidence: SybilGuard uses "human-established trust relations" and defines "attack edges" between honest and sybil regions (Yu et al.).
claims.claim: Sybil defenses can model identities as nodes connected by human-established trust relations and reason about a limited number of attack edges between honest and sybil regions.
claims.title: SybilGuard models trust relations and attack edges between honest and sybil regions
claims.name: SybilGuard models trust relations and attack edges between honest and sybil regions
claims.text: Sybil defenses can model identities as nodes connected by human-established trust relations and reason about a limited number of attack edges between honest and sybil regions.
